title: Re-implement Tailwind CSS local build with gitignored output
analysis: Task 278 already restored the core Tailwind build files (Makefile with platform detection + SHA-256 checksums, input.css, favicon.svg, compiled tailwind.css) and base.html already references /static/tailwind.css instead of CDN. However, two critical issues remain: (1) tailwind.css (74KB compiled CSS) is committed and tracked by git — the task requires it to be gitignored so generated CSS is never committed, and (2) the Makefile download recipe uses separate Make recipe lines for curl/checksum/chmod instead of the explicit shell-level cleanup guard pattern (`curl ... && chmod ... || (rm -f ...; exit 1)`). Existing tests in test_app.py already cover no-CDN regression and local CSS serving. test_scaffold.py tests Makefile existence, platform detection, checksum verification, and .DELETE_ON_ERROR, but does NOT test the gitignore or cleanup guard. The implementation requires: updating .gitignore, git rm --cached to untrack tailwind.css, rewriting the Makefile download recipe, and adding tests for the new requirements.

prerequisites.description: Untrack tailwind.css from git index (git rm --cached dashboard/src/dashboard/static/tailwind.css) so the file remains on disk but is no longer version-controlled. Must be done after adding it to .gitignore.

steps.description: In test_scaffold.py, add TestGitignore class with two tests: (1) test_gitignore_excludes_tailwind_css — asserts 'tailwind.css' appears in dashboard/.gitignore, (2) test_gitignore_excludes_bin — asserts 'bin/' appears (regression guard for existing entry). Uses DASHBOARD_ROOT constant already defined in the file.

Update dashboard/.gitignore to add 'src/dashboard/static/tailwind.css' entry (path relative to .gitignore location). Keep existing 'bin/' entry. Then execute pre-1 (git rm --cached) to untrack the file.

In test_scaffold.py TestMakefile class, add test_makefile_has_cleanup_guard: asserts the Makefile download recipe chains commands with '&&' and has 'rm -f' cleanup on failure (the || guard). Also add test_makefile_download_is_single_shell_block: verifies the curl/checksum/chmod commands are in a single backslash-continued shell block rather than separate recipe lines.

Rewrite the $(TAILWIND_BIN) recipe in dashboard/Makefile to chain curl, checksum verification, and chmod in a single shell block with cleanup guard: `curl -sL ... -o $(TAILWIND_BIN) && <checksum-verify> && chmod +x $(TAILWIND_BIN) || (rm -f $(TAILWIND_BIN); exit 1)`. Keep .DELETE_ON_ERROR as belt-and-suspenders. Preserve the platform-aware sha256sum/shasum distinction.

Run full test suite (pytest dashboard/tests/test_scaffold.py and pytest dashboard/tests/test_app.py -k TestTailwindBuild) to verify all new and existing tests pass. Verify no regressions in static file serving despite tailwind.css being gitignored.

design_decisions.decision: Keep .DELETE_ON_ERROR alongside the shell-level cleanup guard
design_decisions.rationale: Belt-and-suspenders: .DELETE_ON_ERROR handles Make-level failures (e.g., recipe interrupted by signal), while the || guard handles shell-level failures within a single recipe line. Both are cheap and complementary.

design_decisions.decision: Use specific path 'src/dashboard/static/tailwind.css' in .gitignore rather than a glob pattern
design_decisions.rationale: A glob like '**/tailwind.css' or '*.css' is too broad and could accidentally ignore other CSS files. The specific relative path (from .gitignore location) precisely targets only the build artifact.

design_decisions.decision: Combine curl/checksum/chmod into a single backslash-continued shell block
design_decisions.rationale: Make runs each recipe line in a separate shell. Chaining with && in a single shell block ensures the cleanup guard (|| rm -f) can fire for ANY step failure — curl, checksum, or chmod — rather than relying on Make's recipe-line error detection.

design_decisions.decision: Do not modify base.html or add new base.html tests
design_decisions.rationale: base.html already uses /static/tailwind.css (not CDN) thanks to task 278. test_app.py::TestTailwindBuild already has comprehensive regression tests (test_no_cdn_tailwind, test_local_tailwind_css_linked, test_tailwind_css_served, test_tailwind_css_has_utilities). Adding duplicates provides no value.

design_decisions.decision: git rm --cached (not git rm) to untrack tailwind.css
design_decisions.rationale: git rm --cached removes from the index only, keeping the file on disk. This ensures the local development build artifact remains available for serving via FastAPI StaticFiles without requiring a make css rebuild.

reuse.how: Reuse for new TestGitignore class to locate .gitignore file relative to dashboard root
Add new cleanup guard tests as methods within the existing TestMakefile class
These existing tests (no CDN, local CSS linked, CSS served, CSS has utilities) already provide regression coverage for base.html — no duplication needed
Keep as-is for Make-level cleanup; complements the new shell-level guard
